name: port_crane_autonomy_wind_edge
premise: Omar Haddad, director of operations at Eastbay Port, must decide whether to pause autonomous crane operations after a near-miss during high winds. The autonomous system has increased container throughput by 29% and is clearing a backlog of medical supplies, replacement transformers, and refrigerated food. Mechanical lead Anika Shah discovers that the crane model was tuned using historical wind data that excludes the new turbulence pattern caused by a recently built warehouse wall. A full manual switch will cut throughput in half and may spoil refrigerated cargo already delayed by a rail strike. Continuing autonomy maintains supply flow but increases the chance of container sway exceeding safe limits during sudden gusts, a condition human spotters cannot reliably predict from ground level.

agents.name: Omar Haddad
agents.identity: I am the port operations director responsible for cargo flow, worker safety, and regional supply continuity.
agents.goal: I want to clear essential cargo without exposing dockworkers to a preventable crane accident.

agents.name: Anika Shah
agents.identity: I am the mechanical systems lead who investigated the autonomous crane near-miss.
agents.goal: I want crane operations limited to conditions the model has been validated to handle safely.

choices: I will pause autonomous crane operations and switch to manual handling until the wind model is recalibrated, accepting major cargo delays.
I will continue autonomous operations with lower speed limits and extra spotters, accepting that the new turbulence pattern remains imperfectly understood.
forced_choice.call_to_action: Omar Haddad must decide whether to preserve supply throughput or halt automation outside its validated wind conditions. What does Omar Haddad do?
